dailyJudgementSnippet: HN’s strongest emotional signal was negative toward Anthropic because of billing/support complaints, while OpenAI was mixed-to-negative overall; Gemini only appeared briefly, but those mentions cast it as part of the frontier tier Meta still had not joined, giving it the day’s narrowest net-positive edge despite light coverage [E5][E1][E4].
winnerExplanation: Google Gemini wins on net vibe rather than volume. Its coverage was sparse, but the mentions it received were favorable benchmark/comparison mentions with no clear negative counterweight [E4]. OpenAI had more discussion, but it mixed frontier-status credit with criticism of ChatGPT/Codex behavior [E1][E2][E7]. Anthropic had some benchmark prestige, yet the day was dominated by strongly negative complaints about billing and support failures [E3][E5].

openai.judgementSnippet: Mixed, leaning negative: commenters still treated OpenAI/Codex as frontier-level, but ChatGPT drew copyright-extraction criticism and Codex was cited as still missing basic MCP/product expectations [E1][E2][E7].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Negative overall: Anthropic got some prestige as the Opus/Claude benchmark, but that was overwhelmed by angry billing/support complaints and additional privacy/security worries around Claude Code [E3][E5][E6].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Lightly positive but sparse: Gemini was mainly mentioned as one of the frontier names Meta still was not matching, with little else said [E4].

evidence.summary: On 'ML promises to be profoundly weird,' a top comment says ChatGPT will 'slurp' writers’ work and regurgitate it without users ever finding the original article.
In the 'Muse Spark' thread, commenters place 'OAI' and Codex among the small set of frontier AI providers and ecosystems Meta still trails.
In the 'Muse Spark' thread, commenters use Opus 4.6/Claude as the benchmark and call Anthropic the 'leading AI company.'
In the 'Muse Spark' discussion, Gemini is named as part of the 'OAI v Anthropic v Gemini' frontier conversation that Meta still has not entered.
The Anthropic billing/support story includes multiple complaints about lost credits, downgrades, and weeks-long delays with only an AI bot standing between users and human support.
In the vibecoding safety thread, a commenter says Claude can access files outside the working directory and raises long-term data retention/privacy concerns about Anthropic.
In comments on the Anthropic support story, OpenAI is also criticized: Codex is said not to fully implement MCP, with the commenter arguing frontier vendors still fail at basic product reliability.
In the vibecoding thread, a commenter says OpenAI Codex likely has millions of subscribers and few horror stories, but still would not trust it with production databases or environments.
